entries.title: User Guide
entries.content:
User Guide

Welcome to Quarkdown! This is the starting point of your document.

Compiling

- To compile this document, please cd to this file’s parent directory and run:
	quarkdown c main.qd

- 

- To enable live preview, run:
	quarkdown c main.qd -p -w

Wiki

For further information and guides, please check out the official wiki.

Structure

- _setup.qd: global setup, included in each subdocument.
- _nav.qd: navigation tree, displayed in the sidebar.

entries.title: What is CrowdVision?
entries.content:
What is CrowdVision?

Welcome to the CrowdVision User Guide! 👋

> 💡 The Short Version
> CrowdVision is a comprehensive digital twin and crowd analytics platform. It is designed to help you monitor building occupancy, environmental conditions, and safety alerts in real-time.

Whether you are managing a single office floor or an entire campus, CrowdVision brings your physical spaces into the digital world, making it easier than ever to understand how your buildings are being used and to keep everyone inside them safe.

-----

🌟 Core Capabilities

CrowdVision takes complex data and turns it into an intuitive, visual experience. Here is what you can do with the platform:

🏢 3D Digital Twin📊 Analytics Dashboard🔔 Smart Notifications🤖 AI Assistant🛠️ Data ManagementFeatureInteract with a live, 3D digital replica of your buildings and floors. See exactly what is happening, where it is happening.Navigate through beautifully designed dashboards to monitor real-time building occupancy and environmental conditions.Receive instant alerts for critical events, such as overcrowding or high temperatures, so you can act immediately.Leverage artificial intelligence to get quick answers and insights about your building’s data.Easily manage domains, buildings, and room data to keep your digital twin perfectly synced with the real world.Description

-----

👥 Who is CrowdVision For?

CrowdVision is built to be accessible, yet powerful enough for different types of users:

- Facility Managers: Get a bird’s-eye view of building health, optimize space utilization, and respond rapidly to environmental or occupancy alerts.
- Administrators: Manage user access, configure building domains, and customize the digital twin environment.
- General Users: View safe zones, check room availability, and stay informed about the spaces they interact with daily.

> 🔒 Access Note
> While some features of the platform can be viewed as a guest, administrative actions (like editing room details or managing domains) require a registered account.

-----

🚀 Ready to dive in?

Now that you know what CrowdVision is, let’s get you set up and familiar with the platform!

entries.title: How CrowdVision Works
entries.content:
How CrowdVision Works

> 💡 The Big Picture
> CrowdVision seamlessly connects physical spaces to the digital world. By combining real-time sensor data with interactive 3D models, it gives you a complete, live picture of your facilities.

Understanding CrowdVision is easy once you know its four core pillars. Here is a step-by-step look at how the platform helps you manage your spaces.

-----

1. 🏢 Domains: Your Organizational Hub

Everything in CrowdVision is organized into Domains. A domain represents a distinct organization, campus, or environment.

With our Multi-Domain Management feature, you don’t need multiple logins. You can seamlessly switch between different organizations (like a main university campus and a separate engineering faculty) using a single account.

-----

2. 🧊 The 3D Digital Twin: Visualizing Space

Once you select a domain, you can step into the Model View. This is an interactive 3D representation of your building.

Instead of looking at flat floor plans, you can:

- Navigate Freely: Click, drag, and rotate the building to see it from any angle.
- Inspect Rooms: Click on any room to instantly view its layout, capacity, and current status. Selected rooms are highlighted in green for easy tracking.
- Filter by Floor: Isolate specific levels of the building to focus purely on the area that matters most to you.

-----

3. 📊 The Dashboard: Real-Time Analytics

If the Digital Twin is your eyes, the Dashboard is your command center. It translates all the live sensor data into a clean, easy-to-read tabular format.

For every room, CrowdVision automatically calculates an Occupancy Status based on live headcounts vs. maximum capacity:

EmptyNormalCrowdedFull / OvercrowdedStatus0% Occupancy (Green)1% – 50% Occupancy (Blue)51% – 95% Occupancy (Orange)96%+ Occupancy (Red)What it means

> 📺 Pro Tip: Use Focus Mode to cast the dashboard to large screens in lobbies or control rooms for better long-distance readability!

-----

4. 🧠 The AI Assistant: Smart Insights

Stop digging through complex tables. The AI Assistant is integrated directly into the platform to help you manage data using natural language.

Simply click the ✨ Sparkle Icon and ask questions like:

- “Which rooms are currently overcrowded?”
- “What is the average temperature in the Science Block?”

The AI reasons about the data and can even suggest actions, like recommending alternative rooms when one is full.

entries.title: Account Creation
entries.content:
Account Creation

While you can explore some basic features of CrowdVision as a guest, getting the most out of the platform requires a user account. Creating an account allows you to interact with the digital twin, receive notifications, and access organizational data.

-----

🔐 Standard Registration & Login

Getting into the platform is quick and easy.

Creating a New Account

1. Click the Login button in the top navigation bar.
2. At the bottom of the window, click “Don’t have an account? Register”.
3. Enter your desired Username, a valid Email address, and a secure Password.
4. Click Create Account.

> ℹ️ Default Role: By default, all new users are assigned the Standard Customer role. This allows you basic viewing access.

Logging In

If you already have an account, simply open the Login menu, enter your Username and Password, and click Login. Once authenticated, your username will appear in the top navigation bar.

-----

🏢 Joining an Organization (Elevated Roles)

If you are a facility manager, staff member, or administrator, you will need higher permissions than a Standard Customer to edit rooms, view advanced analytics, or manage settings.

CrowdVision handles this seamlessly using an Invite Code or QR Code system.

Using a 6-Digit Invite Code

During the sign-up process, you can easily claim your elevated role if your organization’s admin has provided you with a 6-digit code.

1. Open the Registration window.
2. Fill in your standard details (Username, Email, Password).
3. Click the “I have an invite code” button.
4. A secure input field will appear. Enter the 6-digit code provided by your administrator.
5. Complete your registration.

By doing this, the system will automatically recognize you and grant you the exact permissions you need (such as Business Staff or Business Admin) right from your very first login!

Using Role QR Codes

Alternatively, if you are physically with your administrator or if they have printed onboarding materials, they can generate a specific QR Code directly from their Administration Dashboard.

Administrators have access to a dedicated QR tab where they can select a specific role (e.g., Admin, Business Admin, Business Staff) and generate a unique QR code image. Scanning this code with your device simplifies the process of joining the correct organization with the correct privileges.

> 💡 Tip for Admins: Generating Role QR Codes is a great way to quickly onboard a large group of staff members during a training session without having to read codes aloud!

entries.title: Using the Dashboard
entries.content:
Using the Dashboard

Once you have logged in and selected your organization’s domain, the Dashboard serves as your primary command center for real-time analytics.

Unlike the 3D Digital Twin, which provides a spatial view, the Dashboard gives you a comprehensive tabular overview of all rooms within a selected building at a single glance.

-----

🎛️ Dashboard Interface

At the top of the dashboard, you will find two primary controls:

1. Time & Date: Displayed prominently for quick reference, ensuring you always know you are looking at live, current data.
2. Building Selection: Use the dropdown menu to switch between different buildings within your current domain (for example, switching from “Main Campus” to “Science Block”).
3. Fullscreen Mode Icon: Click this to enter Focus Mode, which optimizes the dashboard for large display screens (more on this later).

-----

📊 The Room Statistics Table

The core of the dashboard is the live data table. It automatically updates to provide real-time metrics for every room in the selected building.

Here is a breakdown of what each column tells you:

RoomStatusTeacherTempPeopleCapacityColumnThe unique identifier, name, or number of the room.A quick-read text indicator of how full the room is (see the logic below).The name of the staff member currently scheduled for that room (if applicable).The current live temperature of the room.The real-time count of occupants currently detected by the room’s sensors.The maximum allowed number of people for the room for safety and comfort.What it means

-----

🚦 Understanding Occupancy Status

To help you identify potential bottlenecks or safety hazards instantly, CrowdVision automatically calculates a status based on the percentage of the room’s capacity currently filled.

Look out for these color-coded indicators:

- 🟢 Empty: 0% Occupancy
- 🔵 Normal: 1% – 50% Occupancy
- 🟠 Crowded: 51% – 95% Occupancy
- 🔴 Full: 96% – 100% Occupancy
- 🚨 Overcrowded: > 100% Occupancy (Requires immediate attention)

-----

📺 Fullscreen Mode (Focus Mode)

If you are setting up CrowdVision on a dedicated display monitor—like in a public lobby, a hallway, or a security control room—you will want to use Fullscreen Mode.

How to use it:

1. Look for the Fullscreen Icon (expand arrows) at the top of the dashboard.
2. Click it to enter Focus Mode.

What happens next?
The interface will immediately hide the side navigation menus and the top bar, expanding the data table to fill the entire screen. The text size is also automatically scaled up, making the dashboard highly readable from a distance.

> 💡 Exiting Fullscreen: To go back to the normal view, simply press the Esc key on your keyboard or click the Fullscreen icon again.

entries.title: The Digital Twin
entries.content:
The Digital Twin

The Model View is the heart of CrowdVision. It offers an interactive 3D representation of your building, allowing you to visually locate rooms, check their physical layout, and manage their underlying properties.

-----

🖱️ Navigating the 3D Scene

Interacting with your building is intuitive and fluid:

- Rotate: Click and drag your mouse anywhere in the scene to rotate the building and view it from different angles.
- Zoom: Scroll up and down with your mouse wheel, or use the on-screen control panel buttons.
- Select a Room: Click directly on any room block to select it. The selected room will instantly turn Green and become semi-transparent so you can easily focus on it.

-----

🎛️ The Control Panel

Located at the bottom center of the screen, the control panel provides quick actions to manipulate the 3D camera:

🧊💥➕➖🎥IconReset ViewFocus on RoomZoom InZoom OutPanorama ModeActionReturns the camera to its default starting position.Also known as “Explode” mode. Isolates the currently selected room by expanding the view, allowing you to see it clearly apart from the rest of the building structure.Moves the camera closer to the structure.Moves the camera further away.Toggles a smooth, automatic rotation of the building—perfect for showcase purposes or wall displays.What it does

-----

🏢 Managing Buildings & Floors (Left Menu)

The sidebar on the left side of the screen is your structural navigator. It allows you to:

1. Select Building: Choose which building structure to visualize from your domain.
2. Filter by Floor: Click on a specific floor number. This will automatically hide all other floors, allowing you to focus purely on that level.

-----

📝 Room Details & Editing (Right Menu)

Whenever you select a room in the 3D view, a dedicated panel appears on the right side of the screen displaying its specific live details.

> 🔒 Permissions Note: Editing room parameters requires higher organizational permissions (such as Business Staff or Admin). Standard Customers can only view the data.

How to edit a room:

1. Select a room in the 3D view.
2. Click the Edit button in the right panel.
3. You can modify the following fields:
	- Room ID: The unique name or number of the room.
	- Capacity: The maximum safe number of people allowed.
	- Max Temperature: The specific threshold that triggers temperature alerts (the default is 28°C).
	- Theme Color: The color used to represent the room visually in charts or panel headers.
4. Click Save to apply the changes instantly.

entries.title: Domain Subscription
entries.content:
Domain Subscription

> 💡 New in v2.0.0: Multi-Domain Management
> CrowdVision now allows you to switch between different organizations seamlessly with a single account. No more juggling multiple logins for different campuses or office buildings!

In CrowdVision, a Domain represents a distinct organization or physical environment (for example, a university campus, a corporate headquarters, or a shopping mall).

Depending on your permissions, you can browse, join, and switch between various domains directly from your dashboard.

-----

🌍 Managing Your Memberships

To manage the organizations you belong to, navigate to the Domain Management page via the main dashboard. Here, you will see a list of all available domains.

Subscribing to a Domain

If a domain is public, you can easily add it to your profile to view its Digital Twin and real-time sensor data.

1. Browse or search for the organization in the Domain List.
2. Click the Subscribe button next to the domain name.
3. You will immediately join as a “Viewer” (Standard Customer) and gain access to their public building data.

> ℹ️ Note on External Logins: If an organization uses an external secure login system (like their own corporate portal), clicking “Subscribe” will securely redirect you to their page to verify your identity before granting access.

Switching Domains

Once you are subscribed to multiple domains, you can switch between them at any time. Selecting a different domain will instantly reload the Dashboard and the 3D Digital Twin to reflect the new organization’s data.

Unsubscribing

Need to clean up your profile? If you no longer need to monitor an organization, locate the domain in your list and click Leave to unsubscribe and remove it from your active view.

-----

🛠️ For Administrators: Creating Domains

If you have administrative privileges, you will also have the ability to Create new domains for your organization.

To do this:

1. Click the + Add button to open the domain creation wizard.
2. Enter the Main Domain identifier (e.g., mycompany.com).
3. Choose how users will authenticate.
4. (Optional) Add subdomains for related branches or departments (e.g., warehouse.mycompany.com).
